vBRIEFInfo.description: Story A of #1378 -- first-class ## Allocation context dispatch-envelope schema (foundation).

plan.title: feat(preamble): first-class ## Allocation context dispatch-envelope schema (#1378 Story A)

narratives.Description: Add a structured `## Allocation context` section to the canonical dispatch envelope (templates/agent-prompt-preamble.md) so downstream skills (#1378 Story B) and the deterministic gate (#1378 Story C) can read the swarm-cohort consent token mechanically instead of inferring it from free-form prose. Foundation story: B and C build against the schema this story freezes.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm dispatcher, I want a canonical structured allocation-context section in the dispatch envelope, so any downstream skill or script can decide whether batched work was operator-approved without prose pattern-matching.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: 1. Add a new `## Allocation context` section to templates/agent-prompt-preamble.md documenting the frozen schema (dispatch_kind, allocation_plan_id, batching_rationale, cohort_vbriefs, operator_approval_evidence) plus a worked example and the recognition contract (swarm-cohort + non-null allocation_plan_id + batching_rationale satisfies the #1371 consent token; absent section falls back to the #1371 prose carve-out). 2. Add tests/content/test_allocation_context_schema.py pinning the section heading, every field name, the example, and the recognition-contract sentence. 3. Add a CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry referencing #1378.
narratives.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1378 Story A. The schema is the canonical reference for #1371's merged swarm-cohort carve-out wording (build SKILL Step 0).

items.title: ## Allocation context schema + example in agent-prompt-preamble.md

narrative.Acceptance: templates/agent-prompt-preamble.md carries a `## Allocation context` section documenting dispatch_kind, allocation_plan_id, batching_rationale, cohort_vbriefs, operator_approval_evidence, with a worked example and the recognition contract naming swarm-cohort + non-null allocation_plan_id + batching_rationale as the satisfied consent token, and the absent-section fallback to the #1371 prose carve-out.
narrative.Traces: Traces to #1378 Story A -- schema section.

items.title: Content test pins the schema documentation

narrative.Acceptance: tests/content/test_allocation_context_schema.py asserts the section heading, all five field names, the example block, and the recognition-contract sentence are present; passes under task check.
narrative.Traces: Traces to #1378 Story A -- content test.

references.title: Issue #1378: first-class allocation-plan reference in dispatch envelope (post #1371 deterministic-tier enabler)

expected_outputs: templates/agent-prompt-preamble.md carries the canonical ## Allocation context schema + example + recognition contract
